pattern: Direct topic search on GitHub yields high relevance for framework discovery tasks
Search exact phrase 'self-evolving AI agent' on GitHub yields high-quality repos with direct relevance
DDGS with Python 3.11 works reliably; add year qualifier (2025) to boost freshness
effect: 30 unique web results including courses, tutorials, and tools not on GitHub

pattern: Use Python subprocess runner instead of shell heredocs in for-loops to avoid background execution hangs
effect: reliability: all 5 platforms completed without hanging
evidence: run_gen1.py succeeded, prior heredoc approach hung

pattern: arXiv 'all:' search for self-evolving agent returns many unrelated papers; consider title-only search for precision
effect: 21 results but ~50% tangential (ASR, colonoscopy, dark energy)

finding: sort=relevance gives 53% pain ratio vs 10% for sort=top
Without restrict_sr=on, search returns r/all results (net neutrality posts etc)
"Claude Code" in quotes = 16,600 score. Without quotes or with modifiers = <50
Abstract terms like 'AI coding agent context' score 26 points. HN users use product names.
Exa found 8 perfect GitHub issues in one query. gh search found 0 for same topic.
impact: Always use Exa for GitHub issue discovery

finding: 'ignores CLAUDE.md' finds pain points. 'CLAUDE.md best practices' finds guides.
impact: Use symptom language for pain point discovery

finding: volume × avg_engagement is gamed by hot posts appearing for every query. NEW_urls × avg_engagement prevents this.
Show HN posts with <100 points are usually self-promotion, not community-validated content
'frustrating', 'hate', 'terrible' don't find pain points. Specific symptom descriptions do.
'I told it' matches success stories (bragging), not complaints
HF API with 3+ keywords returns empty. Keep to 1-2 core terms.
When you find a good dataset, check the author's other datasets and HF Collections that include it
5 consecutive rounds with <10% improvement reliably signals convergence. Previous value of 3 was too aggressive — caused premature stops.
impact: Prevents infinite exploration without cutting short promising runs

finding: xreach search returns empty for all query types tested. Use Exa with site:twitter.com instead.
Queries with proven outcome (intake → WHEN/USE blocks): "AI coding agent" (score=70)
impact: These queries led to repos that produced real WHEN/USE blocks. Boost in future sessions.

finding: Words that correlate with high-scoring queries: claude, agent, best, recommendation, advanced
impact: Use these words when generating queries

finding: Session: 60 queries, 59 winners (98%), 473 unique URLs, confidence=18.1
Session: 2 queries, 2 winners (100%), 53 unique URLs, confidence=None
Session: 1 queries, 1 winners (100%), 26 unique URLs, confidence=None
Words that correlate with high-scoring queries: claude, claude.md, best, practices, techniques
impact: Use these words when generating queries

finding: Session: 60 queries, 58 winners (96%), 941 unique URLs, confidence=22.7
Words that correlate with high-scoring queries: claude, agent, practices, 2026, advanced
impact: Use these words when generating queries

finding: Session: 60 queries, 59 winners (98%), 866 unique URLs, confidence=24.6
Session: 1 queries, 1 winners (100%), 12 unique URLs, confidence=None
Session: 1 queries, 1 winners (100%), 4 unique URLs, confidence=None
Words that correlate with high-scoring queries: claude, best, practices, agent, typescript
impact: Use these words when generating queries

finding: Session: 60 queries, 59 winners (98%), 503 unique URLs, confidence=34.1

pattern: gh search repos with --sort stars yields excellent results for framework discovery; 20 repos from single 'self-evolving agent' query
effect: 51 GitHub repos found, many with 100+ stars

pattern: WebSearch results lack date metadata fields (published_at, created_utc, updated_at). Must explicitly extract and write these for judge.py freshness scoring.
effect: freshness score 0.076 despite most content being recent

pattern: 120s latency budget is too tight for multi-platform research with sequential tool calls. Either parallelize or increase to 300s.
Own-knowledge contributions of foundational works (STaR, Reflexion, Voyager, LATS, CRITIC) add depth that search APIs miss entirely
effect: 12 relevant foundational works added, improving both synthesis quality and evidence diversity

detail: Adding date extraction guidance to llm-evaluate.md improved freshness from 0.076 to 0.660 (+867%). Most results have extractable dates from arXiv IDs, GitHub updatedAt, URL patterns, or snippet text. The evaluator must be explicitly told to extract them.

Restricting gene-query.md to entity-only queries and 30-day recency dropped total score from 0.751 to 0.607. Diversity, relevance, and quantity all suffered. Lesson: query dimension variety (entity-led, pain-led, object-led, context-led) is load-bearing for search quality.

Git commit on improvement + git revert on regression both worked correctly. The AVO safety mechanism prevents bad skill changes from persisting. Commit b933609 kept good change, revert 4fec873 removed bad change.

pattern: Systematic recall before search produces 45+ items on well-known topics, reducing search budget to gap-filling only. 97.7% relevance when queries target gaps.
Chinese-language queries on zhihu surface unique industry trend analysis and Chinese-market perspectives not available in English searches.
effect: 2 unique results from zhihu not found elsewhere
evidence: Self-Evolving as 2026 keyword article, AI self-evolution survey

pattern: For survey-type research, searching for 'survey' + topic yields comprehensive resources quickly. Two major surveys and three domain surveys found in one round.

Search 'self-evolving search agent' on arxiv yields extremely high precision results for self-evolving search topic. Key papers: SE-Search (2603.03293), EvolveSearch (2505.22501), MR-Search (2603.11327)
OpenReview site: search finds ICLR papers not on arxiv yet. SSP (ICLR 2026) found via 'search self-play LLM ICLR 2026' query on web-ddgs.
effect: Found ICLR 2026 accepted papers (SSP, ASL) that are critical for freshness

pattern: Reddit returns zero results for academic ML topics like 'self-evolving search'. Skip Reddit for paper-centric research tasks.

knowledge-growth.json update has high score leverage: updating from neutral values (initial_entries=45) to accurate session values boosted total score from 0.687 to 0.713. Always update knowledge-growth.json with accurate session data.
evidence: judge.py output before and after update

pattern: Freshness cutoff = 183 days ago (Oct 2 for April 3 session). Papers from Aug-Sep 2025 count as 'old'. For 2025-topic sessions, explicitly search for 'October November December 2025' or '2026' to boost freshness.
effect: Freshness 0.544 instead of potential 0.7+ with more targeted recent queries
